Title: The iron–sulfur cluster biosynthesis protein SUFB is required for chlorophyll synthesis, but not phytochrome signaling

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SUF system Fe–S cluster biosynthesis protein; SUFBCD complex subunit
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes chlorophyll biosynthesis via Fe–S cluster biosynthesis and maintenance of Mg-proto MME cyclase activity; not required for phytochrome far-red signaling in this study.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SUFB knockdown or mutant lines show pale-green leaves with reduced chlorophyll a and b. 2. SUFB-deficient plants accumulate Mg-proto MME in light-grown tissues, consistent with inhibition of Mg-proto MME cyclase activity. 3. SUFB overexpression complements the pale phenotype and Mg-proto MME accumulation of laf6 in white light. 4. The far-red long-hypocotyl and dark-grown porphyrin accumulation phenotypes of laf6 are not caused by SUFB deficiency.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: photosynthetic light harvesting and plant growth
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis (hmc1, laf6); Dex-inducible RNAi knockdown; 35S overexpression/complementation; HPLC metabolite profiling of chlorophyll intermediates; immunoblotting
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional depletion phenotypes detected especially in developing leaves of 3- to 4-week-old plants; also assayed in 7-day-old seedlings
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: long-day white light; darkness/etiolation; far-red light; dexamethasone induction of RNAi
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Dex-inducible promoter for RNAi; CaMV 35S promoter for overexpression
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong depletion causes pale-green phenotype and reduced chlorophyll; knockout unavailable due to embryo lethality.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to preserve chloroplast Fe–S cluster biogenesis and chlorophyll formation, but likely unsuitable for simple engineering because strong loss causes severe chlorophyll deficiency and embryo lethality.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The classic laf6 long-hypocotyl phenotype segregates independently from the SUFB-linked chlorophyll phenotype, indicating a second-site mutation. The metabolic effect appears attributable to impaired SUFBCD complex function rather than a unique SUFB-specific moonlighting role.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SUF system Fe–S cluster biosynthesis ATPase subunit; SUFBCD complex component
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal chlorophyll synthesis; SUFC knockdown causes pale-green leaves and Mg-proto MME accumulation similar to SUFB deficiency.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Dex-inducible SUFC RNAi plants are pale green. 2. SUFC-deficient leaves accumulate Mg-proto MME. 3. Results support that intact SUFBC2D complex activity is required for chlorophyll biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: photosynthetic light harvesting and plant growth
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dex-inducible RNAi knockdown; phenotype analysis; HPLC pigment profiling
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining SUFC function is important for chlorophyll production, but downregulation is deleterious.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used mainly to show that the metabolic defect reflects impaired Fe–S cluster synthesis by the SUFBCD complex, not a SUFB-specific secondary function.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SUF system Fe–S cluster biosynthesis scaffold subunit; SUFBCD complex component
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal chlorophyll synthesis; SUFD knockdown causes pale-green leaves and Mg-proto MME accumulation similar to SUFB deficiency.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Dex-inducible SUFD RNAi plants are pale green. 2. SUFD-deficient leaves accumulate Mg-proto MME. 3. These data support that chlorophyll synthesis depends on the SUFBC2D Fe–S cluster biosynthesis complex.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: photosynthetic light harvesting and plant growth
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dex-inducible RNAi knockdown; phenotype analysis; HPLC pigment profiling
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining SUFD activity is important for chloroplast metabolism, but suppression is metabolically harmful.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core experimentally tested component proving that inhibition of Fe–S cluster synthesis is the proximate cause of Mg-proto MME accumulation.
